planTitle: Map bass response to macro motion
summary: Plan involves no architectural changes. All bass-to-macro-motion infrastructure (audio extraction, mapping, camera motion, mesh rotation, shader deformation, synthetic fallback) is already implemented across existing modules. Work is limited to unskipping three tests, fixing stale test assertions from the Canvas2D→Three.js migration, and verifying acceptance criteria. Production code changes are unlikely unless T-018-04 reveals a clamping gap in vertex shaders.

concerns.detail: Skipped tests may reference Canvas2D-era APIs and require updates to work with Three.js BufferGeometry. This is test maintenance, not architectural risk.

Stacked shader deformations (radial expansion × noise × twist × drift) at max bass could push vertices outside frustum. Existing per-effect caps mitigate this, but T-018-04 will confirm. If clamping is needed, it is a localized shader fix, not an architectural change.
Verifying GPU-side displacement in tests may require headless GL context or CPU-side approximation. This is a test tooling concern, not architectural.

claudeMdCompliance.3dRequirement: satisfied — plan preserves existing Three.js perspective camera + volumetric point cloud
claudeMdCompliance.shaderIntegrity: satisfied — plan includes verification of shader compilation and WebGL validation
claudeMdCompliance.audioReactivity: satisfied — bass drives macro deformation, synthetic fallback ensures muted operation
claudeMdCompliance.renderCompletionGate: satisfied — step 5 verifies clean shaders, no WebGL errors, stable first frame
claudeMdCompliance.definitionOfDone: addressed — build, console errors, storage APIs, autoplay, and UI checks covered in steps 4-5

rationale: Zero architectural footprint. The plan correctly identifies that all production infrastructure is in place and scopes work to test verification only. The conditional shader clamping fix (if needed) is a bounded, localized change. No new modules, dependencies, APIs, or cross-cutting concerns are introduced.
